channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This messaging mecca integrates Slack, WhatsApp, and Telegram
item.description: I like chatting with my friends—who doesn’t?—but I don’t always know where to find them. There are simply too many apps. Some of my friends text, others use WhatsApp, while others yet insist on using Discord or the DM feature in whatever random social network they prefer.
item.dc:creator: Justin Pot, Fast Company

item.title: What Sora’s Martin Luther King Jr. problem revealed to OpenAI
item.description: Martin Luther King Jr. did plenty to change the world for the better. And nearly 60 years after his assassination, he’s at the center of a major concession by the world’s leading AI company that puts a battle over intellectual property and the right to control one’s image into the spotlight.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Salesforce chief says he no longer backs Trump sending National Guard to San Francisco
item.description: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff apologized Friday for comments suggesting that President Trump should deploy the National Guard to San Francisco, saying he no longer believes it is necessary.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Maine Senate candidate faces more resurfaced Reddit posts
item.description: Reports have found additional controversial social media posts Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner made years before his bid to oust Sen. Susan Collins (R).
item.dc:creator: Surina Venkat, The Hill

item.title: From heart health to health equity, Dr. David Goff is shaping science and inspiring public service
item.description: "We've had a lot of success, but we're now having backsliding, and there's a lot of work to be done," said Dr. David Goff. The post From heart health to health equity, Dr. David Goff is shaping science and inspiring public service first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: For October’s Patch Tuesday, a scary number of fixes
item.description: Microsoft this week released 175 updates affecting Windows and Office and .NET, including server-based updates for Microsoft SQL Server and Exchange server.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to watch the Orionid meteor shower, debris of Halley's comet
item.description: The Orionid meteor shower peaks this year with the arrival of a new moon, offering a rare chance to see shooting stars without moonlight interference.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Big Tech is paying millions to train teachers on AI, in a push to bring chatbots into classrooms
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — On a scorching hot Saturday in San Antonio, dozens of teachers traded a day off for a glimpse of the future. The topic of the day’s workshop: enhancing instruction with artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia, China crank up AI-powered cyberattacks on the U.S., Microsoft warns
item.description: Russia, China, Iran, and North Korea have sharply increased their use of artificial intelligence to deceive people online and mount cyberattacks against the United States, according to new research from Microsoft.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Unions sue Trump administration over social media vetting of visa holders
item.description: Three labor unions sued the Trump administration Thursday in connection with a program that searched social media posts of visa holders, arguing that it violates the First Amendment.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Major companies, including Google and Dior, hit by massive Salesforce data breach
item.description: Google, Dior, Allianz and dozens of other companies lost sensitive customer data in Salesforce-related breaches affecting millions of records across multiple sectors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple adds game-changing iPhone document feature that users waited years to see
item.description: Apple launches iOS 26 with new Preview app that combines document editing, PDF annotation and scanning features into one streamlined iPhone experience.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Booker courts red state voters online, urging them to join ‘No Kings’ protests
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) is pressing red state voters to join local “No Kings” protests Saturday and put pressure on their GOP legislators to end the government shutdown through his outreach with social media influencers and creators in Florida and North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft: Russia, China increasingly using AI to escalate cyberattacks on the US
item.description: Russia, China, Iran and North Korea are using artificial intelligence to improve their cyberattacks on U.S. companies, governments and individuals, according to new research from Microsoft.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Death of kidnapped South Korean student spurs talks with Cambodia to tackle online scams
item.description: PHNOM PENH, Cambodia (AP) — Cambodian and South Korean officials agreed Thursday to cooperate in combating online scams, after the death of a South Korean student who was reportedly forced to work in a scam center in Cambodia triggered public outrage in South Korea.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and SOPHENG CHEANG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Apple’s Vision Pro: the Newton of the XR age
item.description: Apple’s alleged decision to suspend development of a lighter and cheaper Vision Pro device sounds a lot worse than it actually is, because in a short time the product will be seen as the Newton of the AR age. Why the Newton? Vision Pro is ahead of its time.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Musk’s $56 billion Tesla payday fight hits Delaware Supreme Court
item.description: Elon Musk‘s lawyers will urge the Delaware Supreme Court on Wednesday to restore his $56 billion pay package from Tesla, as one of the biggest corporate legal battles enters its final stage nearly two years after a lower court judge rescinded the Tesla CEO’s record compensation.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia’s DGX Spark desktop supercomputer is on sale now, but hard to find
item.description: Nvidia’s “personal AI supercomputer,” the DGX Spark, may run fast but it’s been slow getting here. It finally went on sale on Oct.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 7 newer data science tools you should be using with Python
item.description: Python’s rich ecosystem of data science tools is a big draw for users. The only downside of such a broad and deep collection is that sometimes the best tools can get overlooked. Here’s a rundown of some of the best newer or less-known data science projects available for Python.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: US indicts 'mastermind' of Cambodian cyberscam network, seizes $15B in Bitcoin
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) announced Tuesday it has indicted the alleged orchestrator of a Cambodia-based cryptocurrency fraud scheme. The indictment, filed Oct.
item.dc:creator: Max Rego, The Hill

item.title: It's called automated officiating. The NBA is utilizing it to get even more calls right
item.description: The play, in real time to the naked eye, might have looked very close to a violation. LeBron James leaped, got his right hand on the ball with a few tenths of the game’s final second remaining and tapped it through the basket to give the Los Angeles Lakers a buzzer-beating win last season.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: SpaceX launches the 11th test flight of its mega Starship rocket with another win
item.description: SpaceX launched another of its mammoth Starship rockets on a test flight Monday, successfully making it halfway around the world while releasing mock satellites like last time. Starship — the biggest and most powerful rocket ever built — thundered into the evening sky from the southern tip of Texas.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: OpenAI partners with Broadcom to design its own AI chips
item.description: OpenAI said Monday it is working with chipmaker Broadcom to design its own artificial intelligence computer chips. The two California companies didn't disclose the financial terms of the deal but said they will start deploying the new racks of customized “AI accelerators” late next year.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
